fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Michigan Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Michigan - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity michigan, how to transfer physical therapist license to michigan, physical therapist compact michigan, michigan physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: Michigan LARA — Physical Therapy
board.parent_dept: Michigan Department of Licensing and Regulatory Affairs (LARA)
board.mailing_address: Department of Licensing and Regulatory Affairs Bureau of Professional Licensing P.O. Box 30670 Lansing, MI 48909

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in Michigan.

steps.title: Create a MiPLUS Account
steps.description: Navigate to the MiPLUS portal at michigan.gov/miplus and register for a new user account if you don't already have one.

steps.title: Initiate an Application
steps.description: Log into your MiPLUS account, select 'New Application' from the dashboard, and choose 'Physical Therapist' as your profession.

steps.title: Complete Application Sections
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the online application, providing personal details, educational history from a CAPTE-accredited program, and professional background.

steps.title: Upload Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Electronically submit or arrange for the submission of official transcripts, NPTE scores (if applicable), and verification of licensure from any state where you hold or have held a PT license directly through MiPLUS or by mail if required (e.g., official transcripts sent directly from institution).

steps.title: Submit Payment
steps.description: Pay the required application fee of $150 via credit card through the MiPLUS system to finalize your application submission.

steps.title: Monitor Application Status
steps.description: Regularly check your application status within your MiPLUS account and promptly respond to any requests for further information from the Michigan Board of Physical Therapy.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Michigan physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Michigan have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. Michigan participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Michigan without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to Michigan?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Michigan while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in Michigan
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in Michigan, users must create an account and submit their application through the Michigan Professional Licensing User System (MiPLUS).
